events.preview: Feb 5 resolved the Moscow officer question as Vladimir Alekseyev, outside the prior set. I’m scanning Feb 5 articles for tomorrow’s Narges Mohammadi and Washington Post questions, plus the Feb 7-Feb 13 group.

blocks.text: Feb 5 resolved the Moscow officer question as Vladimir Alekseyev, outside the prior set. I’m scanning Feb 5 articles for tomorrow’s Narges Mohammadi and Washington Post questions, plus the Feb 7-Feb 13 group.

/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os
path='articles/2026/02/05/articles.jsonl'
print('exists', os.path.exists(path))
if not os.path.exists(path): raise SystemExit
lines=0; usable=0; bydate={}
for line in open(path):
    lines+=1
    a=json.loads(line); dp=a.get('date_publish') or ''
    if dp <= '2026-02-05': usable+=1
    bydate[dp]=bydate.get(dp,0)+1
print('lines',lines,'usable<=Feb5',usable,'latest_dates', sorted(bydate)[-10:])
checks={
'198725 Narges exile': lambda t: 'narges mohammadi' in t and ('exile' in t or 'sentenced' in t or 'zanjan' in t or 'qazvin' in t or 'semnan' in t or 'tabriz' in t),
'456284 WaPo publisher': lambda t: ('washington post' in t and 'will lewis' in t) or ('publisher' in t and 'washington post' in t),
'180958 Super Bowl': lambda t: ('super bowl' in t and ('seahawks' in t or 'patriots' in t or 'seattle' in t or 'new england' in t)),
'534224 downhill women': lambda t: ('women' in t and 'downhill' in t and ('olympic' in t or 'milan-cortina' in t or 'cortina' in t)) or ('vonn' in t and 'olympic' in t),
'630740 Lebanon collapse': lambda t: ('leban' in t and ('building collapse' in t or 'residential building' in t) and ('15' in t or 'dead' in t or 'killed' in t)),
'318661 Colombia peace': lambda t: ('colombia' in t and 'peace talks' in t) or ('eln' in t and 'peace talks' in t),
'14689 school shooting country': lambda t: ('secondary school' in t and 'shooting' in t) or ('school shooting' in t and ('germany' in t or 'sweden' in t or 'canada' in t or 'france' in t or 'austria' in t)),
'739916 carrier': lambda t: ('uss abraham lincoln' in t and ('carrier' in t or 'strike group' in t)) or ('aircraft carrier' in t and 'middle east' in t),
'398988 Lebanon abduction': lambda t: ('israel' in t and 'abducted' in t and 'lebanon' in t) or ('southern lebanon' in t and 'official' in t and 'abducted' in t),
'312764 Egypt planning': lambda t: ('egypt' in t and 'planning minister' in t) or ('minister of planning' in t and 'egypt' in t),
'527803 Canada school town': lambda t: ('school shooting' in t and 'canada' in t) or ('la loche' in t and 'shooting' in t),
'795774 French activist': lambda t: ('far-right activist' in t and 'france' in t) or ('macron' in t and 'meloni' in t and ('killed' in t or 'activist' in t)),
'538697 Gaza troops': lambda t: ('8000 troops' in t or '8,000 troops' in t or ('troops' in t and 'gaza' in t and ('ready' in t or 'june' in t))),
'487245 address tension': lambda t: ('address' in t and 'president' in t and ('iran' in t or 'venezuela' in t or 'china' in t or 'russia' in t)),
'745180 Cyclone Gezani': lambda t: 'gezani' in t or ('madagascar' in t and 'cyclone' in t),
'613203 Tractor AFC': lambda t: ('tractor' in t and ('afc' in t or 'champions league' in t or 'playoff' in t)),
'652747 Navalny toxin': lambda t: ('navalny' in t and ('toxin' in t or 'novichok' in t or 'body' in t or 'tissue' in t)),
'304287 Bangladesh opposition': lambda t: ('bangladesh' in t and ('opposition' in t or 'election' in t or 'parliament' in t)) or 'jamaat-e-islami' in t,
'901381 rail cables': lambda t: ('burned cables' in t or ('high-speed rail' in t and ('olympics' in t or 'winter olympics' in t or 'cables' in t)))
}
arts=[]
for line in open(path):
    a=json.loads(line)
    if (a.get('date_publish') or '')>'2026-02-05': continue
    title=a.get('title') or ''; c=a.get('content') or ''
    t=(title+'\\n'+c).lower()
    arts.append((a,title,c,t))
for label,fn in checks.items():
    hits=[]
    for a,title,c,t in arts:
        if fn(t):
            idx=0
            for kw in ['narges','washington post','super bowl','downhill','building collapse','peace talks','school shooting','abraham lincoln','abducted','planning minister','canada','far-right','gaza','address','gezani','tractor','navalny','bangladesh','high-speed rail']:
                j=t.find(kw)
                if j"'!=-1:
                    idx=j; break
            snippet=(title+'"' | '+c[max(0,idx-350):idx+1300]).replace('\\n',' ')
            hits.append((a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),snippet[:1800]))
            if len(hits)>=8: break
    print('\\n##',label,'hits',len(hits))
    for dp,src,sn in hits:
        print('-',dp,src,sn)
PY"

The Post, which gained legendary status when its reporting helped bring down president Richard Nixon in the Watergate scandal, will see "substantial" reductions in its newsroom, Executive Editor Matt Murray said. The shrinking of the Post comes as major traditional media outlets in the United States face intense pressure from President Donald Trump, who routinely denigrates journalists as "fake news" and has launched multiple lawsuits over coverage of his presidency. Bezos, one of the world's richest people, has become close to Trump in the Republican's second term. His Amazon behemoth controversially paid Trump's wife, First Lady Melania Trump, a reported $40 million for a documentary this year, along with another $35 million for marketing. Murray said the shifts at the Post reflect the radically changing economy for news media. This "will help to secure our future ... and provide us stability moving forward," Murray said in a note to employees. The Post announced it is shuttering the sports desk in its current form, dialing back its international footprint, shrinking its Metro staff and eliminating its books section. Murray, who personally delivered the staffing shakeup to the paper's employees on a Zoom webinar, defended the Amazon founder as critics say he could afford to save the jobs that were cut. - 2026-02-05 www.independent.co.uk ‘Bloodbath’ at the Washington Post as roughly one-third of the staff is laid off by Jeff Bezos | The Washington Post told staffers today that it was moving forward with a sweeping round of layoffs that was part of a “broad strategic reset” of the storied newspaper, which will include eliminating the sports desk, severely cutting back on its international coverage, dismantling its books section, and restructuring its local news team. Though it was not immediately known exactly how many employees would be impacted by the cuts, it had previously been reported that about 100 staffers from the newsroom could be laid off, and as many as 300 employees from the broader company would face terminations. However, after emails were sent to staffers on Wednesday morning, it is expected that roughly one-third of staff — about 300 of the roughly 800 journalists in the newsroom — will be laid off, with some staffers saying that this was a “bloodbath”. “This ranks among the darkest days in the history of one of the world’s greatest news organizations,” the Post’s former executive editor Marty Baron said Wednesday. “The Washington Post’s ambitions will be sharply diminished, its talented and brave staff will be further depleted, and the public will be denied the ground-level, fact-based reporting in our communities and around the world that is needed more than ever.” The announcement, which was made by the Post

- 2026-02-05 www.thenation.com Mamdani Touts a Stunning View of the City, but Struggles to Clear the Sidewalks | olds non-white politicians to a higher standard, and the perils of neglecting the details of day-to-day governance for the distractions of soaring vision and lofty rhetoric. As a spectacle, the mayor’s rooftop extravaganza was first-rate; he even promised, ahead of the Super Bowl, “We will never light this building up for the Patriots.” But at the end of his first month on the job, during which he was repeatedly asked—and declined—to grade his own performance, it would be difficult to justify anything higher than an A-. The Mamdani administration was admirably efficient in meeting the headline task of clearing the streets from late January’s monster snow storm—a forbidding and at times fatal hurdle for some new mayors. But the city’s bus stops, crosswalks, and sidewalks were another story. Not all of this was the mayor’s fault. Property owners, not the city, are responsible for maintaining safe sidewalks; bus stops with shelters are supposed to be shoveled out by JC Decaux (the advertising agency); crosswalks near city parks are the responsibility of the parks department. Nor was it the mayor’s, or the city’s, fault that some New Yorkers died in the cold. In 2022, the last year for which figures are available on the city’s data portal, 54 city residents perished as a result of exposure to the cold—a sharp rise from 34 the previous year, and at a time when the Adams administration was aggressively clearing homeless encampments from the city’s streets. But a mayor needs to be able to walk and chew gum at the same time. Given the ample advance warning, not just of the historic scale of the blizzard but of the likely duration 
- 2026-02-05 www.fastcompany.com These 5 brands are winning the Super Bowl pregame push | Let’s rewind for a 10-second Super Bowl ad history lesson that goes like this: In 2011, Volkswagen decided to drop its full ad—called “The Force”—online the Wednesday before the Super Bowl. If you’re confused as to why this happens, don’t sweat it, even Christopher Walken wasn’t sure in BMW’s 2024 Super Bowl teaser. Super Bowl commercials are no longer just Super Bowl commercials. They are Super Bowl campaigns that run for weeks before and after the game. Now, say it in your best Walken voice, “Why would they do that?” So much more than $8M The last time the Patriots and Seahawks met in the Super Bowl in 2015, 30 seconds of commercial time on NBC went for about $4.4 million. This year, a 30-second spot averaged $8 million, plus another $8 million in required spend for other NBC sports broadcasting and the 2026 Milan Cortina Winter Olympics, according to Ad Age. That price tag could go as high as $10 million, when you consider expanding that to Peacock and Telemundo. And that’s all before you spend a dime on actually making the ad. - 2026-02-05 www.irishtimes.com Why was coach Bill Belichick snubbed from the NFL Hall of Fame? | nd get a big panel to tell you if you’re good or not. It’s the way that it works unfortunately.” Barely two years have passed since Belichick stepped down as head coach of the New England Patriots, after nearly a quarter of a century of unparalleled success, a dynasty that yielded six Super Bowls for a club that previously had none. At 71 years old, it looked like he could retire having set benchmarks unlikely to ever be passed, or perhaps have a final fling as soon as some NFL contender inevitably offered him big bucks to take one more shot at reaching the big dance. Inexplicably, that kind of opportunity never knocked and instead, to continue coaching, he had to take a gig way down the college football food chain at the University of North Carolina. By the time he fetched up at Chapel Hill in December, 2024, Belichick – the NFL eminence grise – had become a figure of fun to some. This was due to his high-profile relationship with Jordon Hudson, a woman who is 49 years his junior. Their romance played out in the full, unforgiving glare of the social media spotlight. Suddenly, the most taciturn coach in American sport cut an unmoored, Learesque figure. Here he was frolicking on beaches in mermaid photoshoots, doing the walk of shame on Ring cameras and becoming nightly comic cannon fodder for stand-ups. Their unlikely coupling continues to be the tabloid gift that keeps on giving. Even as almost all gridiron folk agreed Belichick had been done a grievous wrong by the Hall of Fame, it emerged Hudson intends to throw him a huge party on the day next August when the annual induction takes place. input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import csv,json,os
from collections import Counter
print('state_exists', os.path.exists('state.json'))
if os.path.exists('state.json'):
    print(json.dumps(json.load(open('state.json')), indent=2)[:5000])
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('market.csv', newline='')))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='false']
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='true']
print('rows',len(rows),'active',len(active),'resolved',len(resolved))
print('active_without_prediction',sum(1 for r in active if not r['my_prediction'] or r['my_prediction']=='None'))
print('active_pred_dates',Counter(r['my_prediction_date'] for r in active).most_common(35))
print('\\nResolved on/after 2026-02-05:')
for r in resolved:
    if r['resolution_date']>='2026-02-05':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'],'GT',r['ground_truth'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
print('\\nActive upcoming <=2026-02-14:')
for r in active:
    if r['resolution_date'] <= '2026-02-14':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
PY"
